perturbation.original_text: Racing agrees that it will actively participate in the Rolex 24 at Daytona in January, 2010.
perturbation.changed_text: Racing will consider participating in racing events but makes no guarantees to participate in the Rolex 24 at Daytona in January, 2010.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Racing agrees that it will actively participate' to 'Racing will consider participating,' it creates uncertainty about Racing's commitment to a key event. This contradicts the original intent of securing participation in a specific race. In Section 2(ii), the contract mentions 'those facilities designated or assigned for the use of the Racing Team at each race and race location at which the Racing Team actually participates in the race' implying Racing Team participation. But, with the change, Racing Team participation in a race is not guaranteed, and will create an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to payment by the Sponsor of the sponsorship fee provided for herein, during the term of this Agreement Racing shall cause the Racing Team to provide for the Sponsor's benefit all of the benefits customarily associated with the sponsorship of a Daytona Prototype Series racing team and consistent with the benefits provided to the Sponsor in 2005 - 2009 (individually, a "Benefit," and collectively, the "Benefits"), including but not limited to the following: (i) displaying prominent identification of the Sponsor's name and/or logo in signage on the race car and racing suits and, where appropriate, on other team equipment (subject to approval by the Sponsor); (ii) making available for the use of the Sponsor (x) the personalities associated with the Racing Team, including without limitation the name, voice, picture, portrait, likeness, persona and/or signature of each driver for endorsements, commercial advertising and promotions in any and all media throughout the world during the term of this Agreement, (y) the Racing Team's home base facilities in Texas, and (z) those facilities designated or assigned for the use of the Racing Team at each race and race location at which the Racing Team actually participates in the race, all for appropriate public relations and other promotional and marketing purposes. Racing will consider participating in racing events but makes no guarantees to participate in the Rolex 24 at Daytona in January, 2010. As it concerns (y) and (z) above, access shall be subject to appropriate security and safety restrictions designated by the applicable racing location and the Racing Team; (iii) making available for the use of the Sponsor a non-racing look-alike (a "Show Car") of the GAINSCO 99 race car (the "Car") used by the Racing Team. Subject to the Sponsor's first right to use the Show Car, it will also be made available to Racing when such use does not interfere with the Sponsor's use of the Show Car; (iv) allowing the Sponsor the use of the likeness of the Car, including all paint and graphics, for promotion and advertising of or by the Sponsor, and Racing shall be responsible for all necessary consents and permissions from any other sponsors to be sure the Sponsor can use the likeness of the Car as specified herein; (v) prohibiting the endorsement by Racing and any members of Racing, including the drivers, of any entities, products or services which are in direct competition or otherwise inconsistent with the Sponsor or it products or services, unless such endorsement activity is approved in writing by Racing and the Sponsor; and (vi) allowing the Sponsor to use the conference room and other areas of the racing shop and garage for meetings and similar events, provided that the Sponsor gives prior notice of the need for such use, and such use does not interfere with operations of the racing shop and garage and is otherwise consistent with reasonable requirements imposed by Racing to assure orderly operations and provide for adequate safety measures at all times.
perturbation.justification: The original text committed Racing to participate in the Rolex 24 at Daytona. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating Racing will 'consider participating' but 'makes no guarantees'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines benefits provided to the Sponsor based on Racing Team's participation in races, including facilities at each race location (section (ii)(z)). If Racing doesn't guarantee participation, the Sponsor's expected benefits are undermined, creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Sponsor shall pay to Racing a sponsorship fee in the amount of $750,000.00 for the term of this Agreement, payable in an initial installment payable on or before February 1, 2010 in the amount of $350,000.00 and ten installments of $40,000.00 on or before the first day of each month commencing March 1, 2010 and ending with the installment due on December 1, 2010 (unless this Agreement is sooner terminated pursuant to Section 14 hereof, in which case Sponsor shall have no obligation to make any payments after the date of termination).
perturbation.changed_text: The Sponsor shall pay to Racing a sponsorship fee as determined by mutual agreement on a quarterly basis. Payment amounts and schedules will be documented separately and attached as Exhibit A.
perturbation.explanation: The specific amount and payment schedule in Section 3 are replaced with a vague agreement for quarterly payments, creating an in-text contradiction. The reference to 'Exhibit A' is problematic if Exhibit A does not exist or does not clearly define the payment schedule. This undermines the 'Sponsorship Fee' in section 2 that states, 'Subject to payment by the Sponsor of the sponsorship fee provided for herein'. The original clarity is lost, now creating a dependency on a potentially nonexistent or incomplete Exhibit A. Without a defined fee, providing benefits as per section 2 is uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to payment by the Sponsor of the sponsorship fee provided for herein, during the term of this Agreement Racing shall cause the Racing Team to provide for the Sponsor's benefit all of the benefits customarily associated with the sponsorship of a Daytona Prototype Series racing team and consistent with the benefits provided to the Sponsor in 2005 - 2009 (individually, a "Benefit," and collectively, the "Benefits"), including but not limited to the following: (i) displaying prominent identification of the Sponsor's name and/or logo in signage on the race car and racing suits and, where appropriate, on other team equipment (subject to approval by the Sponsor); (ii) making available for the use of the Sponsor (x) the personalities associated with the Racing Team, including without limitation the name, voice, picture, portrait, likeness, persona and/or signature of each driver for endorsements, commercial advertising and promotions in any and all media throughout the world during the term of this Agreement, (y) the Racing Team's home base facilities in Texas, and (z) those facilities designated or assigned for the use of the Racing Team at each race and race location at which the Racing Team actually participates in the race, all for appropriate public relations and other promotional and marketing purposes. Racing will consider participating in racing events but makes no guarantees to participate in the Rolex 24 at Daytona in January, 2010. As it concerns (y) and (z) above, access shall be subject to appropriate security and safety restrictions designated by the applicable racing location and the Racing Team; (iii) making available for the use of the Sponsor a non-racing look-alike (a "Show Car") of the GAINSCO 99 race car (the "Car") used by the Racing Team. Subject to the Sponsor's first right to use the Show Car, it will also be made available to Racing when such use does not interfere with the Sponsor's use of the Show Car; (iv) allowing the Sponsor the use of the likeness of the Car, including all paint and graphics, for promotion and advertising of or by the Sponsor, and Racing shall be responsible for all necessary consents and permissions from any other sponsors to be sure the Sponsor can use the likeness of the Car as specified herein; (v) prohibiting the endorsement by Racing and any members of Racing, including the drivers, of any entities, products or services which are in direct competition or otherwise inconsistent with the Sponsor or it products or services, unless such endorsement activity is approved in writing by Racing and the Sponsor; and (vi) allowing the Sponsor to use the conference room and other areas of the racing shop and garage for meetings and similar events, provided that the Sponsor gives prior notice of the need for such use, and such use does not interfere with operations of the racing shop and garage and is otherwise consistent with reasonable requirements imposed by Racing to assure orderly operations and provide for adequate safety measures at all times.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined a specific sponsorship fee and payment schedule. The changed text replaces this with a vague agreement for quarterly payments to be documented in 'Exhibit A'. The contradicted text states that the benefits provided by Racing are 'Subject to payment by the Sponsor of the sponsorship fee provided for herein'. By removing the defined fee and schedule, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text, as the sponsorship fee is no longer clearly defined, making the provision of benefits uncertain and creating a dependency on a potentially nonexistent or incomplete Exhibit A.

perturbation.original_text: Racing agrees to indemnify the Sponsor and its officers, directors, agents and employees and to hold them harmless from any loss, claim, cost, damage or liability in excess of the Maximum Obligation which (i) the Sponsor shall incur as a result of this Agreement, or (ii) arises from any failure by Racing to perform any of its obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Racing agrees to indemnify the Sponsor and its officers, directors, agents and employees and to hold them harmless from any loss, claim, cost, damage or liability which arises from any failure by Racing to perform any of its obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'in excess of the Maximum Obligation', this change deletes a crucial limitation on Racing's indemnification responsibility to the Sponsor. Since the 'Maximum Obligation' is tied to sponsorship fees which has been altered in Section 3, the deletion in section 5, contradicts with the mention in section 3 (if the Exhibit A is not present).
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Sponsor shall pay to Racing a sponsorship fee as determined by mutual agreement on a quarterly basis. Payment amounts and schedules will be documented separately and attached as Exhibit A.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited Racing's indemnification obligation to the Sponsor to amounts 'in excess of the Maximum Obligation'. The changed text removes this limitation, making Racing potentially liable for the full amount of any loss, claim, cost, damage, or liability. This contradicts the contradicted text (Section 3), which states the sponsorship fee is determined by mutual agreement and documented in Exhibit A (the 'Maximum Obligation'). If the sponsorship fee (maximum obligation) is changed or if Exhibit A is not present, the removal of the limitation creates a direct contradiction, as Racing's potential liability is now undefined and possibly unlimited, conflicting with the prior agreement tying it to sponsorship fees.
